title: Effective harnesses for long-running agents
body_md: Compaction alone is not enough. We describe an initializer + coding-agent split, with a portable `progress.md` and a strict feature-list JSON, that let Claude Code run productively for days on a single task. Sub-agents take ownership of fresh windows; the lead agent never sees raw artifacts.

title: Notes on Anthropic's harness paper
body_md: Anthropic's new piece on long-running agents is the clearest articulation yet of why a single Claude loop with infinite context is the wrong abstraction. The interesting bit isn't the file-as-memory pattern — it's the strict feature-list scaffold preventing premature 'done.'

title: Sora 3 video generation now available in the API
body_md: Sora 3 lands in the OpenAI API today with longer clips, multi-shot composition, and a new safety classifier. Available for Tier-3+ accounts.

title: letta v1.4.0 — MemFS GA
body_md: MemFS — agent memory projected as git-backed files manipulated through generic computer-use tools — graduates from preview to GA. Replaces bespoke memory_block tools for new agents; existing agents can opt in via `migrate-memfs`.

title: LangGraph 1.2: durable interrupts and per-node checkpointing modes
body_md: 1.2 ships per-node checkpoint modes (sync / async / exit / never), so cheap nodes don't pay for storage they don't need. Durable interrupts let you `interrupt()` anywhere, including inside a tool call, with full state hydration on resume.

title: Claude Code now ships in JetBrains as a native IDE integration
body_md: Native plugin for IntelliJ, PyCharm, GoLand, and Rider. All Claude Code features available, no terminal required.

title: OpenAI Agents SDK on Temporal: tracing, replay, and zero-token retries
body_md: A walkthrough of the GA `OpenAIAgentsPlugin`. Each LLM call becomes a recorded Activity; on retry the cached response is replayed from history at zero token cost. Includes a debugger workflow showing how to time-travel an agent run.

title: pydantic-ai 1.86 — Restate executor + improved Temporal plugin
body_md: New `pydantic_ai.durable_exec.restate` executor (parity with the existing Temporal/DBOS/Prefect plugins). Streaming now works correctly inside all four durable wrappers. Type narrowing on tool outputs improved.

title: ChatGPT introduces persistent shopping carts across sessions
body_md: Shopping conversations now persist a unified cart across sessions and devices. Powered by the new long-running Responses + Conversations stack.

title: Datasette 1.2 with native SQLite vector search
body_md: Datasette 1.2 ships first-class vector search using SQLite's new `vector_search` extension. Includes a built-in embeddings UI for any text column.

title: Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills
body_md: We've made Agent Skills an open standard. A SKILL.md plus optional executable scripts and reference docs; progressive disclosure means only the metadata loads upfront. Now portable across Claude.ai, Claude Code, Agent SDK, and Managed Agents.

title: langgraph 1.2.1 — Postgres saver perf, fix for interrupt() inside subgraphs
body_md: PostgresSaver checkpoint write down ~40% on average. Fixes a bug where `interrupt()` inside a subgraph would not propagate the resume Command upward.

title: Agents SDK adds resumable sandbox sessions
body_md: Sandbox agents now have first-class resume across crashes. A manifest declares which files persist; the new Runner abstract base class lets durable executors plug in. Companion Temporal plugin ships at the same time.

title: The lethal trifecta is everywhere: another MCP supply-chain incident
body_md: A community MCP server combined private repo access, untrusted issue text, and outbound HTTP. Predictable result: prompt injection exfiltrating tokens. The lethal trifecta is exactly as dangerous as advertised.

title: Ambient agents: Temporal as the substrate for always-on agents
body_md: We introduce 'ambient agents' as the pattern for agents that react to event streams rather than running continuously. A single Workflow per user, signals as the input plane, child workflows as task scopes.

title: New: Claude in Sheets
body_md: Add a `=CLAUDE()` formula to any Google Sheets cell. Available to Claude Pro and Team users today.

title: letta v1.4.1 — MemFS bugfixes, Postgres connection pool tuning
body_md: Fixes a race condition in MemFS where two concurrent tools could overwrite each other's edits. Default Postgres pool sized to CPU count; previously hard-coded to 4.

title: Five RAG patterns for production
body_md: Hybrid search, query rewriting, parent-document retrieval, graph-augmented retrieval, contextual compression. Examples in plain LangChain — no LangGraph required.

title: Effective context engineering for AI agents
body_md: Compaction, structured note-taking, sub-agent isolation, and just-in-time retrieval. We share the patterns we use internally to keep Claude productive past the point where naive context-stuffing collapses.

title: Voice mode for ChatGPT teachers
body_md: ChatGPT for Education adds voice mode tailored for K-12 instructors, with classroom-safe defaults.

title: pydantic-ai 1.86.1 — bugfixes for streaming + Temporal
body_md: Fixes a regression where streaming inside a TemporalAgent would buffer entire messages instead of emitting deltas. Also pins anthropic>=0.62 due to a tool_choice schema change.

title: Cost engineering for long-running AI workflows
body_md: How we cut a customer's weekly bill by 6× on a multi-day research workflow: prompt cache discipline (1-hour beta TTL), Sonnet routing for classification steps, and aggressive `continue_as_new` to keep histories small.

title: Quick benchmark: prompt cache hit rates across long agent runs
body_md: I ran the same multi-step research agent twice — once with strict 5-min cache discipline, once with the 1-hour beta TTL. The difference for a 4-hour run was 11× on the input-token bill. Notes and full ledger inside.

title: langgraph 1.2.2 — cron jobs in OSS, no Platform required
body_md: Cron jobs (`@cron('0 9 * * *')` decorator) now usable from OSS LangGraph against any Postgres-backed checkpointer. Previously Platform-only. Includes a small CLI for listing and cancelling scheduled runs.

title: Managed Agents — pricing reduced and waitlist opened
body_md: Runtime price reduced from $0.08 to $0.06 per agent-hour. Waitlist for the next 5,000 customers opens today.

title: letta v1.5.0 — first-class HITL approval primitives
body_md: New `requires_approval` flag on tools and `letta_client.approvals.respond(approval_id, decision)` API. Approvals are stored in Postgres and survive restarts indefinitely.

title: Operator handoff between web and desktop now in beta
body_md: Hand a running Operator session from your phone to your desktop without losing context. Beta to Pro users this week.

title: Autonomous context compression: a year-in-review
body_md: What we've learned shipping LangGraph's autonomous compression in 2025: dynamic thresholds beat static ones, recall-first dominates relevance-first, and most teams under-invest in eval for their compaction step.

title: Building effective evaluators for long-running agents
body_md: If you can't evaluate a multi-day agent end-to-end, you don't know if it's working. We share the evaluator patterns we use: episode replay, factuality probes, and the 'goal coherence' rubric.

title: Temporal Cloud: 99.99% SLA for AI workloads
body_md: Temporal Cloud is now offered with a 99.99% SLA on the AI/Agents tier. Includes priority support for OpenAI Agents SDK, Pydantic AI, Vercel AI SDK, and Google ADK integrations.

title: MCP servers I'm running on a tiny VPS
body_md: Eight MCP servers, $5/month VPS, ~30MB RAM each. The lift on long-running agents from having durable, addressable tools is bigger than I expected. Bash transcripts inside.

title: Pricing change: standard input tokens drop 20% across all GPT-5 models
body_md: Effective immediately, standard input token pricing for all GPT-5 family models is 20% lower. Cached input pricing unchanged. Full price table in the API docs.

title: pydantic-ai 1.87.0 — graph snapshots and partial replay
body_md: `pydantic_graph` now ships first-class snapshots: any node entry persists a snapshot to your durable backend. `graph.replay(snapshot_id, modify=lambda g: ...)` lets you fork from any past point. Works with all four durable executors.

title: Case study: a 30-day literature-review agent on LangGraph + DynamoDB
body_md: A research lab built a literature-review agent that runs continuously for 30 days, reading papers and updating a synthesis. End-to-end: LangGraph + DynamoDB checkpointer + scheduled cron + LangMem for procedural memory. Findings, costs, and lessons inside.

title: Sub-agents in the Claude Agent SDK: best practices after 6 months
body_md: What we've learned watching customers use sub-agents at scale: keep sub-agent prompts under 500 tokens, never spawn recursively, and always require a structured return type. The third rule is the one most often violated.

title: letta v1.5.1 — bugfix for HITL deadlock under concurrent approvals
body_md: Fixes a deadlock when multiple approval requests on the same agent are answered out of order. Adds an `approvals.list(agent_id, status='pending')` helper.

title: Replay debugging: pinpoint LLM non-determinism with one click
body_md: Walkthrough of the new Replay Debugger UI in Temporal Cloud: pick any past Workflow execution, mutate a single LLM response in the recorded history, and see the divergence ripple through.

title: ChatGPT Pets
body_md: A persistent virtual companion in ChatGPT, with 12 species and a feeding mechanic. Available to Plus subscribers.

title: langgraph 1.3.0 — Store gets vector + graph backends
body_md: The long-term Store now ships first-class vector and graph backends in addition to KV. Pgvector, Pinecone, Neo4j, and FalkorDB integrations included. Memory namespaces stable.

title: I let an agent maintain my reading list for two weeks
body_md: Two weeks of letting Claude Agent SDK keep my reading list. Approval gate every Sunday. Findings: it learned my tastes faster than I expected, drifted toward 'Anthropic-coded' content without correction, and burned more cache than it should have. Ledger and prompts inside.
